response.justification: This candidate falls significantly short of the role's core requirements. While demonstrating strong communication and software engineering practices, the resume shows only ~6 months of documented C++ experience (versus mandatory 5-10 years), no Windows platform expertise, and zero exposure to financial services or capital markets domains. The candidate's career has been predominantly focused on web development, infrastructure, and D-language projects—a fundamentally different technical stack from what the role demands.
strengths: Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing demonstrated through multiple talks, workshops, and open-source projects with clear documentation
Strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD pipeline setup, and infrastructure knowledge (Docker, deployment systems)
Relevant secondary skills including Java development and SQL experience that could support financial-systems roles
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ experience: only ~6 months documented (National Instruments) versus mandatory 5-10 years requirement
No Windows platform development experience evident; career shows predominant Linux and web-based development focus
Zero financial services domain exposure: no fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets experience despite this being a core hiring criterion

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (6 months vs. 5-10 years required)
No financial services or capital markets domain knowledge
Strong software engineering practices and communication skills
